---
title: "Offshore Wind Infrastructure Model Assumptions"
---

This encyclopedia documents the methodology and techno-economic assumptions used to model offshore wind infrastructure, electricity export, offshore hydrogen production, and hydrogen transport.

The site is organised as an assumptions manual. Each component page bundles the modelling choices that matter for that component:

- cost model, including CAPEX, installation cost, O&M, and scaling
- energy balance, including conversion efficiency, auxiliary power, and losses
- availability treatment on an annual energy basis
- sizing, mass, geometry, and other drivers that affect costs elsewhere in the value chain
- source basis, uncertainty, and architecture applicability

The emphasis is deliberately methodological. Results, scenario outcomes, and interpretation of model output are out of scope for this encyclopedia.

## Main Sections

- [Methodology](methodology/overview.qmd)
- [Architectures](architectures/overview.qmd)
- [Wind Resource and Layout](wind_resource_and_layout/overview.qmd)
- [Turbine System](turbine_system/overview.qmd)
- [Electrical Infrastructure](electrical_infra/overview.qmd)
- [Hydrogen Production](hydrogen_production/overview.qmd)
- [Hydrogen Infrastructure](hydrogen_infra/overview.qmd)
- [Platforms](platforms/overview.qmd)
- [Offshore Installation](offshore_installation/overview.qmd)
- [Shared Model Inputs](shared_model_inputs/overview.qmd)
